steps.title: Cross multiply safely
steps.content:
The equation is undefined at $x=0$, $x=1$, and $x=-1$, so those values cannot be solutions. Cross multiply the original equation:
$$
\frac{x}{(x-1)(x+1)}=\frac{81}{56x}
$$
$$
56x^2=81(x-1)(x+1)
$$

steps.title: Solve the equation
steps.content:
Use the difference of squares:
$$
56x^2=81(x^2-1)
$$
$$
56x^2=81x^2-81
$$
$$
25x^2=81
$$
$$
x=\pm\frac{9}{5}
$$
Among the choices, only the positive value appears.

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content: Graphing or solving the original equation directly gives the same positive choice. The answer is D.
